code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Calculate RSI
    rsi = ta.momentum.RSIIndicator(df['close'], window=params['rsi_window']).rsi()
    
    # Identify support/resistance levels
    rolling_low = df['low'].rolling(window=params['sr_window']).min()
    rolling_high = df['high'].rolling(window=params['sr_window']).max()
    
    # Generate signals
    signals = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Long when price bounces off support with RSI oversold
    signals[(df['close'] <= rolling_low * (1 + params['sr_threshold'])) & 
            (rsi <= params['rsi_low'])] = 1
    
    # Short when price rejects resistance with RSI overbought
    signals[(df['close'] >= rolling_high * (1 - params['sr_threshold'])) & 
            (rsi >= params['rsi_high'])] = -1
    
    return signals.astype(int)
rationale: GBP/USD exhibits strong reactions at support/resistance levels when confirmed by RSI extremes, capturing reversal opportunities in ranging markets.
